commit.message:
fix: strip prompt echo from output and fix new-session detection

Bug 1 (prompt echo in live tab):
The Copilot SDK prepends <current_datetime> to assistant messages, and
gpt-5-mini sometimes echoes the instruction headers (Language:, Format:,
Task:) verbatim at the start of its response instead of only outputting
the translated content.

- Add StripTranslationOutput() in translator/prompt.go: strips XML tags
  (e.g. <current_datetime>) and leading prompt-header echoes starting
  with 'Language:' / '言語:' from AI output before display.
- Apply StripTranslationOutput() in buildReasoningLines() and
  buildHistoryBlock() in tui/viewer.go.
- Strengthen RTSystemPrompt() and HistSystemPrompt() to explicitly
  instruct the model not to reproduce instruction headers in output.
- Add unit tests for StripTranslationOutput covering English, Japanese,
  and combined echo patterns.

Bug 2 (new-session detection/alert not firing):
New sessions were first detected as *inactive* (lock file not yet
created) and immediately added to knownSessionIDs. When the lock file
was written shortly after, the session was already "known" so the
active-session alert was never shown.

- Fix updateKnownSessions() in tui/app.go: inactive new sessions are no
  longer added to knownSessionIDs on first sight. They are held out of
  the map until seen as active, allowing the alert to fire correctly on
  the state transition from inactive → active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(vscode_parser): cache VSCodeLogSummary to avoid O(N) re-aggregation

Add a module-level _VSCODE_SUMMARY_CACHE that stores the most recent
VSCodeLogSummary keyed by a frozenset of (path, file_id) tuples.
When all discovered log files have unchanged identities (mtime_ns,
size), the cached summary is returned immediately — reducing per-call
cost from O(total_requests) to O(num_files).

Adds three deterministic tests verifying the cache skips
_update_vscode_summary on warm hits and invalidates correctly on
file changes and new files.

Closes #789

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: claude-code architecture patterns — interactive TUI, command registry, memory discovery, 5-layer permissions

Major refactor inspired by codeaashu/claude-code:

Interactive TUI (prompt_toolkit + ANSI selector):
- Real-time slash command autocomplete as you type /
- Arrow-key ↑↓ navigation for all menus (model, lang, history, commands)
- ESC to cancel any selection, Enter to confirm
- No more number-based input for options

Command Registry (core/command_registry.py):
- Typed Command dataclass with name, aliases, category, enabled
- Fuzzy search with weighted scoring (name 3x, alias 2x, desc 0.5x)
- Prefix bonus for exact start matches

Memory Discovery (core/memory.py):
- Auto-discovers JSHADOW.md files (user-global, project-level, CWD)
- Injects persistent memory into system prompt automatically
- Hierarchy: ~/.jshadow/JSHADOW.md > .jshadow/JSHADOW.md > ./JSHADOW.md

5-Layer Permission System (core/permissions.py):
- Layer 1: Tool-level (read_only tools always allowed)
- Layer 2: Deny rules (fail-closed explicit blocks)
- Layer 3: Allow rules (glob patterns for auto-approve)
- Layer 4: YOLO mode (global auto-approve)
- Layer 5: Manual prompt (ask user)
- PermissionDecision dataclass with behavior/reason/source

BaseTool Upgrades (tools/base.py):
- Added execute() method on BaseTool (preferred pattern)
- New fields: read_only, concurrent_safe, max_result_chars
- ToolResult and ToolContext dataclasses

Spinner Phases (ui/phases.py):
- Phase enum: thinking → streaming → tool_input → tool_use → done
- PhaseIndicator singleton for tracking activity

Streaming Fix:
- Removed spinner+streaming conflict (spinner fights sys.stdout)
- StreamingDisplay: inline tokens only, no double panel rendering
- Added abort() method for error handling
- Removed orphaned duplicate code in orchestrator

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: exempt agent instructions from workspace subpath filter (#307937)

* fix: exempt agent instructions from workspace subpath filter

AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and copilot-instructions.md live at the workspace
root by design. The CLI harness restricts items to workspaceSubpaths
(.github, .copilot, .agents, .claude) which filtered out these root-level
agent instruction files. Now items with groupKey 'agent-instructions' are
exempt from the subpath filter, matching the existing exemption for
instructionFileFilter patterns.

* sessions: include AGENTS.md in instructions listing

The sessions AI customization tree view and overview were only calling
listPromptFiles(PromptsType.instructions) to discover instruction files.
However, AGENTS.md (along with CLAUDE.md and copilot-instructions.md)
is classified as an agent type by getPromptFileType(), so it was never
returned by that call.

Fix by also calling listAgentInstructions() and merging the results
(deduplicating by URI), matching the pattern already used by the
workbench customization editor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: pin Aspire DCP ports in launchSettings for local dev

Aspire 13.2.1 randomizes ports by default. Add
DcpPublisher__RandomizePorts=false to both AppHost launchSettings
so local dev uses the fixed ports from launchSettings.json
(API: 5100, Web: 4200). CI already had this via workflow env var.

No Aspire downgrade needed.

feat: SomaMind Phase A — Router + Digital DNA + REPL commands

Add confidence-gated router (soma_router.h/.c):
- Reflex arc: instant responses for system/safety/greetings (no inference)
- Domain classification: SYSTEM/POLICY/CHAT/CODE/MATH/CREATIVE
- Routing: REFLEX → INTERNAL → EXTERNAL → DUAL (speculative)
- Runtime stats + live threshold tuning

Add Digital DNA (soma_dna.h/.c):
- 128-byte packed struct with cognition parameters
- Dual Core params: Solar (logic, low temp) + Lunar (creative, high temp)
- Meta-evolution: mutate DNA with bounded perturbation + lineage tracking
- Reproduction: copy + mutate + set parent hash (FNV-1a)

Integrate into God File:
- SomaMind initialized at boot (router + DNA defaults)
- REPL commands: /soma_status, /soma_dna, /soma_mutate, /soma_route
- Model topology awareness (external_model_ready synced with SSM state)

New files:
  engine/ssm/soma_router.h   — Router + Reflex arc API
  engine/ssm/soma_router.c   — Router + Reflex implementation
  engine/ssm/soma_dna.h      — Digital DNA structure + API
  engine/ssm/soma_dna.c      — DNA init/validate/mutate/reproduce

instead of using Next.js API routes.

- IdeaList.tsx: Call /api/ideas (Next.js route) instead of backend directly
- Remove NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL environment variable references
- Now all backend calls go through Next.js server → backend

This ensures:
✅ Proper proxy through Next.js server
✅ Environment variables work correctly
✅ No CORS issues
✅ Backend URL not exposed to client

commit.message:
Fix unhandled promise rejection in InsertNewPageDialog.openDialog() (#776)

After PR #774 modified `page-chat-panel.ts`, the frontend CI exited with
code 1 despite all extension tests passing — because `web-test-runner`
was reporting a failing assertion in `page-chat-panel.test.ts` (tests
still expected the old `error`-setting behavior, not the new
`pendingMessage` queuing behavior). That test fix landed in PR #773.

This PR addresses a second issue visible in CI logs from both the
failing and now-passing runs: an unhandled `TypeError` fired from a
fire-and-forget `.then()` in `openDialog()`.

## Root cause

`automagic-identifier-input` is only type-imported in
`insert-new-page-dialog.ts`, so in tests the custom element is never
upgraded. The shadow DOM query returns an unupgraded `HTMLElement`,
which has no `focusTitleInput` method. The existing `?.` guard only
protected against `null`/`undefined` — not against the property being
`undefined` on a live element:

```typescript
// Before — throws TypeError if element not upgraded
this.updateComplete.then(() => {
  const identifierInput = this.shadowRoot?.querySelector<AutomagicIdentifierInput>('automagic-identifier-input');
  identifierInput?.focusTitleInput();  // 💥 not a function
});
```

## Fix

Replace the fire-and-forget `.then()` with `await this.updateComplete`
inside the `async` method, and use optional-call syntax (`?.()`) to
defensively handle the unupgraded case:

```typescript
// After — awaited, no unhandled rejection, safe if not upgraded
await this.updateComplete;
const identifierInput = this.shadowRoot?.querySelector<AutomagicIdentifierInput>('automagic-identifier-input');
identifierInput?.focusTitleInput?.();
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: brendanjerwin <3039+brendanjerwin@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: Remove Procfile and add explicit start command to railway.json

When using Dockerfile, Procfile should not exist as it conflicts with Docker.
Railway will use the Dockerfile CMD which runs 'npm start'.
Added explicit startCommand to railway.json for clarity.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Improve: Add health check and better startup configuration to Dockerfile

- Added HEALTHCHECK for container health monitoring
- Clarified PORT 3000 for Railway
- Better comments for clarity
- ENTRYPOINT + CMD pattern ensures proper signal handling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Force rebuild: Clear Railway cache

Railway seems to be using cached configuration.
This change forces a fresh build and deployment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

infra: explicitly enable public network access on all resources

Add publicNetworkAccess: 'Enabled' to resources that were missing it,
ensuring Bicep redeployments re-enable access if a security tool
disables it.

Resources updated:
- Cosmos DB (global.bicep) — publicNetworkAccess
- HelloAgents Storage Account (stamp.bicep) — publicNetworkAccess
- AI Storage Account (ai.bicep) — publicNetworkAccess
- Key Vault (ai.bicep) — publicNetworkAccess
- Global Log Analytics (global.bicep) — publicNetworkAccessForIngestion/Query
- Regional Log Analytics (region.bicep) — publicNetworkAccessForIngestion/Query
- Monitor Workspace (region.bicep) — publicNetworkAccess

Already had it: ACR, App Insights, AI Services, AI Hub, AI Project.

commit.message:
fix(e2e): use .first() for discussion test — agent may respond multiple times

With a real LLM, the agent responds to both the seed message AND the
discuss trigger, so the agent name appears twice in chat. Use .first()
to match the first occurrence.

All 26 tests pass with LM Studio (liquid/lfm2.5-1.2b).

fix: correct top-p nucleus sampling + add SSM smoke test

- Fix sort-free top-p: accumulate nucleus mass first, then sample within
  clamped cutoff (was sampling with biased target = r * top_p)
- Handle top_p >= 1.0 as full-distribution sampling
- Lower threshold 0.01 -> 0.005 to include more candidate tokens
- Add tests/ssm-autorun.txt: autorun script for SSM inference testing
- Add tests/qemu-ssm-smoke.ps1: QEMU smoke test with pass/fail checks

feat: apply BO  W/D/CC fields, TN=answers , NP/MD modalities

Based on business owner questionnaire answers:

1. W = Week (text field), D = Doctor (text field), CC = Claim Created
   (checkbox). Previously W+D was a single 'WD Verified' boolean and
   CC was 'Copay Collected'. Split into 3 proper fields on checkout
   form, PDF sign sheet, and visit history tables.

   mapper for treatment records column display.

to visits table in Supabase.
